text.title: capacity-bottleneck-checklist Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the capacity-bottleneck-checklist methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent claims 'as documented in the runbook' with no runbook path.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Re-run with explicit input list pinned in context.

antipattern.root-cause: Pressure to avoid naming a single person.
antipattern.fix: Block output. Require a named human or role-with-rotation.

antipattern.symptom: rationale full of 'obviously', 'clearly', no input values cited.
antipattern.root-cause: Rationale written to defend, not to explain.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite citing at least one specific input value or constraint.

antipattern.symptom: headroom_pct accepted at 12% to avoid escalation.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wants to ship without escalating.
antipattern.fix: Reject; route to escalation per r5.

antipattern.symptom: decision changed but version stayed at 1.0.0.
antipattern.fix: Bump version (patch for typo, minor for new input, major for changed decision).
